data.title: As an engineer running debug-everything, I can configure a list of GHA workflows so CI failures are automatically tracked as bug tickets before the diagnostic or fix cycle begins
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Step 0 (GitHub Actions Pre-Scan) to debug-everything's SKILL.md; add debug.gha_workflows and debug.gha_scan_enabled config keys to dso-config.conf with documentation; define the GHA scanning sub-agent instructions in a shared prompt file referenced from Step 0.

**Why**: Engineers and Claude sessions running /dso:debug-everything must currently create bug tickets for CI failures manually before the skill can discover and fix them. This story eliminates that manual step by scanning configured workflows on session entry and auto-creating bug tickets for any untracked failures.

**Scope**:
- IN: Step 0 section in debug-everything SKILL.md (placed before the open-bug-count pre-check); GHA scanning sub-agent instructions (shared prompt file or inline); debug.gha_workflows config key (comma-separated workflow file names); debug.gha_scan_enabled config key (boolean, default true); tag-based dedup using gha:<workflow-file-name>; pre-flight tool probe (per_page=1 list call); graceful degradation with distinguishable log signals; compact summary schema (workflows_checked, tickets_created, failures_already_tracked); orchestrator writes summary as comment on active epic ticket
- OUT: Mid-cycle refresh between fix batches (Story 2); job-level failure attribution; dynamic workflow discovery (config list is the only source; no fallback to auto-discovery if empty)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, debug-everything SKILL.md includes a Step 0 before the open-bug-count pre-check that reads debug.gha_workflows from dso-config.conf; when the key is absent or empty, Step 0 logs "GHA scan skipped: no workflows configured" and proceeds immediately without dispatching any sub-agent
  <- Satisfies: "On entry to debug-everything, a sub-agent reads debug.gha_workflows... If debug.gha_workflows is absent or empty, logs 'GHA scan skipped: no workflows configured' and continues."
- When this story is complete, Step 0 dispatches a haiku sub-agent that performs a pre-flight probe (per_page=1 list call); if the probe returns tool-not-found or an error, the sub-agent emits "GHA scan unavailable: workflow run tools not registered" and exits cleanly; if the probe succeeds, it processes each workflow in the list
  <- Satisfies: SC1 (pre-flight probe) + SC4 (distinguishable unavailable vs no-failures signal)
- When this story is complete, for each workflow in debug.gha_workflows the sub-agent checks whether an open bug ticket carrying the tag gha:<workflow-file-name> already exists; if yes it skips; otherwise it fetches the first page of completed runs and, if the most recent shows a non-success conclusion (failure, timed_out, cancelled, or startup_failure), creates a bug ticket tagged gha:<workflow-file-name> with the workflow file name in the title; if no completed run is found on page 1, it logs "GHA scan: no completed run found for <workflow>" and skips
  <- Satisfies: SC1 (pre-flight probe, tag dedup, ticket creation, no-result handling)
- When this story is complete, the sub-agent returns only a compact summary object (fields: workflows_checked, tickets_created, failures_already_tracked) to the orchestrator; full MCP API response bodies remain in sub-agent context only; the orchestrator writes the summary as a comment on the active epic ticket immediately after the sub-agent returns
  <- Satisfies: SC3 (compact summary + epic comment)
- When this story is complete, setting debug.gha_scan_enabled=false in dso-config.conf causes Step 0 to be skipped entirely with no sub-agent dispatch and no ticket operations; all other debug-everything behavior is unaffected
  <- Satisfies: SC5 (opt-out config key)
- When this story is complete, running debug-everything with debug.gha_workflows=<failing-workflow>.yml produces (a) a new bug ticket tagged gha:<workflow>.yml with the workflow file name in the title, and (b) a GHA scan summary comment on the active epic ticket with workflows_checked >= 1 and tickets_created >= 1, including the new ticket ID, summary line verbatim, and the workflow file name (dogfooding verification)
  <- Satisfies: SC6 (dogfooding verification)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  <- TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Testing] Sub-agent dispatch is not unit-testable via standard test harness. Behavioral tests should verify the sub-agent prompt structure (required sections present: pre-flight probe logic, tag dedup check, compact summary schema) rather than live dispatch outcomes. A structural boundary test (verify required sections exist in the prompt file) satisfies the behavioral testing standard.
- [Reliability] Run-level CI status only (not job-level); the conclusion field on completed runs determines failure. States indicating failure: failure, timed_out, cancelled, startup_failure. States indicating non-failure: success, skipped, neutral. Known limitation: job-level failure attribution is a follow-on story.
- [Maintainability] Risk Register item 4: the GHA scanning sub-agent prompt must be defined once in a shared prompt file (e.g., plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/prompts/gha-scanner.md) and referenced from both Step 0 and the mid-cycle refresh (Story 2). Duplication between the two dispatch points causes drift and is explicitly out of scope for each individual story.
- [Reliability] Repository name derivation (from git remote origin) may fail in fork setups. Document this as a known limitation in the debug.gha_workflows config key description.
- [Integration] NOT verified: GitHub MCP workflow run tools are present in the current session environment. Pre-flight probe handles absence gracefully. Integration testing requires a live environment with these tools registered. No sandbox available.
- [Integration] Verified: GitHub MCP server standard tool names are list_workflow_runs_for_a_workflow (per workflow) and list_workflow_runs_for_a_repository (repo-wide). Either may be used; prefer the per-workflow variant for precision.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed autonomously; pause and ask only when genuinely stuck or facing an unresolvable ambiguity. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
